findings.title: Phases listed out of numerical order
findings.snippet:
In Phase 1, we compute a preliminary partition using a greedy approach.
In Phase 3, we refine the partition using local search.
In Phase 2, we merge small clusters.
findings.explanation: The three phases are presented in the order 1, 3, 2 with no stated reason for the reordering. This reads as a copy-paste or editing error and confuses the logical sequence of the framework.
findings.fix:
Reorder the paragraphs to Phase 1, Phase 2, Phase 3:
"In Phase 1, we compute a preliminary partition using a greedy approach.
In Phase 2, we merge small clusters.
In Phase 3, we refine the partition using local search."

findings.title: Reference to a table that is explicitly absent
findings.snippet: Table~1 shows the results but we were unable to include it due to space constraints.
findings.explanation: The sentence simultaneously references Table 1 and states it was not included. This is self-contradictory; the reader is directed to content that does not exist.
findings.fix: Either include the table or remove the reference entirely. If results must be summarized in prose, replace with, e.g.: "Due to space constraints, we summarize the main results in the text."

findings.title: Unqualified comparative claim
findings.explanation: "Better" is used without specifying better than what, by which metric, or in what setting. The sentence conveys no actionable information.
findings.fix: Provide a qualified claim, e.g.: "Our algorithm achieves lower partition cost than existing greedy methods on standard benchmarks."

findings.title: Missing comma before coordinating conjunction (table sentence)
findings.snippet: Table~1 shows the results but we were unable to include it due to space constraints.
findings.explanation: When "but" joins two independent clauses, a comma is required before the conjunction.
findings.fix: "Table~1 shows the results, but we were unable to include it due to space constraints."

findings.title: Missing comma before coordinating conjunction (theorem sentence)
findings.snippet: We do not prove this theorem formally but it seems intuitively obvious from the structure of the algorithm.
findings.explanation: Two independent clauses are joined by "but" without a preceding comma.
findings.fix: "We do not prove this theorem formally, but it seems intuitively obvious from the structure of the algorithm."

findings.title: Circular justification of significance
findings.snippet: Our contribution is significant because graph optimization is important.
findings.explanation: The sentence is vacuous: it asserts significance solely by restating that the problem domain is important, providing no concrete justification.
findings.fix: State a specific reason, e.g.: "Our contribution is significant because it reduces partitioning cost by up to 15\% on large-scale social-network graphs."

findings.title: Vague reference "this" in related work
findings.snippet: Jones et al.\ (2021) extended this to weighted graphs.
findings.explanation: "this" could refer to the method, the problem formulation, or the partitioning result of Smith et al. A more explicit antecedent improves clarity.
findings.fix: "Jones et al.\ (2021) extended Smith et al.'s partitioning method to weighted graphs."
